title: anemone trifolia subsp. albida
intro: Anemone trifolia subsp. albida, also known as white wood anemone, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the anemone genus and is native to Europe. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Anemone trifolia subsp. albida include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate rheumatic pains, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The herbal preparations of this plant can be used in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it can also be applied topically to the affected area as a poultice.
